faq.question: What is a good NFL passer rating?
faq.answer: A rating of 100+ is elite, 90-99 is excellent, 80-89 is good, 70-79 is average, and below 70 is poor. The all-time record for a season is 122.5 by Aaron Rodgers in 2011. Perfect rating is 158.3.

faq.question: How is passer rating calculated?
faq.answer: The NFL formula uses four components: completion percentage, yards per attempt, touchdown percentage, and interception percentage. Each is scaled and capped at 2.375, then averaged and multiplied by 100.

faq.question: What is the difference between NFL and NCAA passer rating?
faq.answer: The NCAA uses a different formula that produces higher numbers (scale of 0-1000+). An NCAA rating of 150 is roughly equivalent to an NFL rating of 100. The two systems are not directly comparable.

faq.question: Is the Quarterback Rating Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard sports analytics formulas trusted by athletes, coaches, and fans. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Quarterback Rating Calculator?
sections.body: The Quarterback Rating Calculator is a free online tool designed for athletes, coaches, and fans who need quick, accurate calculations in the sports analytics space. By entering your completions, attempts, passing yards, you get instant results including passer rating, performance grade, completion %. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting passer rating right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In sports analytics, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by athletes, coaches, and fans worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to analyze performance with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your completions and need to find the right passer rating. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Completions
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your completions — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: attempts, passing yards, touchdowns, interceptions. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your passer rating, performance grade, completion %. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your completions before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
